data.title: AMD & Nvidia (NVDA) Stocks Slips Despite Microsoft's Commitment to Buy AI Chips
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Nvidia (NVDA) stocks were down on Friday despite positive comments from Microsoft (MSFT) CEO Satya Nadella about artificial intelligence (AI) chip purchases. According to Nadella, Microsoft has "a great partnership with Nvidia, with AMD" and that "because we can vertically integrate doesn't mean we just only vertically integrate."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nadella's comments are a positive for AMD and Nvidia, which have provided the company with chips for its AI efforts. They show that Microsoft is dedicated to still using components from the two companies, despite Microsoft's development of its own AI hardware. That includes the Maia 200, a special AI chip that will be used by the company's Superintelligence team.

While many companies have rushed to develop AI chips of their own, Microsoft's stance shows that there's still plenty of demand for chips from AMD and Nvidia. That makes sense, as these two companies have a long history of developing graphics processing units (GPUs), which are the powerhouses behind AI.

AMD & Nvidia Stock Movements Today

AMD stock was down 3.88% on Friday but was still up 13.21% year-to-date. The stock has also gained 117.49% over the past 12 months, largely due to increased AI demand.

Nvidia stock slipped 0.29% on Friday but was still up 2.81% year-to-date. The stock has also climbed 60.33% over the past 12 months, also alongside rising AI demand.
data.author: William White

data.title: S&P 500 Stumbles on Concerning PPI Inflation Data
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) is trading lower on Friday following a disappointing inflation update from the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The U.S. Producer Price Index (PPI) rose 0.5% month-over-month in December and 3% year-over-year. Both figures were above the consensus estimates of 0.2% and 2.8%, respectively. The PPI tracks the prices that producers charge for their goods and services and is often used as an early indicator of inflation.

Annual Core PPI Growth Reaches 6-Month High

Core PPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, notched a monthly gain of 0.7% while growing 3.3% annually, the highest since July 2025. Again, both figures tallied above their estimates of 0.2% and 2.9%, respectively.

The rise in PPI was mostly attributed to a monthly increase of 0.7% in services, while goods prices remained steady. Margins for final demand trade services jumped 1.7%, accounting for about two-thirds of the increase in services.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Kevin Warsh Isn't 'Always Hawkish,' Says Billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller
data.description:
President Trump's decision to nominate former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh to succeed Jerome Powell came as a surprise to some, as Warsh is known as a "hawk," or someone who favors higher interest rates to keep inflation under control. However, that assumption isn't entirely accurate, according to billionaire and former hedge fund manager Stanley Druckenmiller.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"The branding of Kevin as someone who's always hawkish is not correct," Druckenmiller told the Financial Times on Friday. "I've seen him go both ways."

Druckenmiller Backs Warsh, Flags AI Inflation Risk

Since stepping down from the Fed in 2011, Warsh has served as a partner at Druckenmiller's Duquesne family office. Druckenmiller noted that Warsh was "all in" on lowering rates during the Financial Crisis, and he also supported rate cuts during the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic.

"I could not think of a single other individual on the planet better equipped," he added.

However, Druckenmiller said that one challenge Warsh will face, if confirmed by the Senate, is handling AI-related economic growth without creating additional inflation.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: SanDisk Stock Soars on Q2 Earnings Beat — Buy SNDK or Wait?
data.description:
SanDisk (SNDK) stock jumped sharply after the company delivered a standout Q2 earnings report and guidance, blowing past Wall Street estimates on both profit and revenue. Having said that, the stock's solid rally raises the risk of near-term volatility as some investors lock in profits. For long-term investors who believe AI-driven storage demand is still in the early innings, any pullbacks could present attractive entry points. Overall, SNDK remains compelling for patient investors—but chasing the stock immediately after a big spike may require caution.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

SNDK stock is up roughly 14% in early trading on Friday. Year-to-date, the shares are up more than 125%.

AI Boom Powers SanDisk Profit and Revenue Surge

SanDisk's fiscal second-quarter profit jumped 672% year-over-year to $803 million, or $5.15 per share. Meanwhile, revenue climbed 61% to $3.03 billion, beating analysts' $2.69 billion estimate.

The company's data-center revenue jumped 76% from a year earlier, as artificial intelligence continued to drive a sharp increase in demand.

What Lies Ahead for SanDisk?

Looking ahead, the company said it expects revenue of $4.4 billion to $4.8 billion for the next quarter, far above Wall Street's $2.9 billion estimate. Even the midpoint of the range implies nearly 60% upside versus expectations. SanDisk also guided for adjusted EPS of $12 to $14, compared with analysts' forecasts of just $5.11—a gap of more than 150%.

SanDisk's outlook underscores just how quickly AI-driven demand is accelerating, suggesting that earnings momentum is still building rather than peaking. Despite the strong quarter, management said the company was unable to fully meet surging demand and expects this supply-demand imbalance to persist into 2026. That level of visibility supports continued revenue growth and sustained pricing power.

At the same time, SanDisk's sharp improvement in profitability signals that operating leverage is kicking in as volumes rise.

What Is the Price Target for SanDisk?

Wall Street analysts currently rate SanDisk stock a Moderate Buy, with 10 Buy ratings and four Holds issued over the past three months. After the recent rally, the shares are now trading above SanDisk's average price target of $395.93, implying a downside of 26.6% from current levels.

That said, these ratings and targets are likely to change as analysts update their forecasts following the latest earnings report.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: CL Earnings: Colgate-Palmolive Stock Skyrockets over 5% as Sales Growth Beats Estimates on LATAM Rebound
data.description:
Colgate-Palmolive (CL) reported fourth-quarter results on Friday that indicated a bounceback in its core oral care business. The consumer goods giant posted organic sales growth of 2.2%, comfortably beating the 1.4% growth projected by Wall Street analysts. While the company dealt with a massive non-cash impairment charge of $794 million related to its skin health business, its "Base Business" earnings per share climbed 4% to $0.95, signaling that the company's primary brands remain resilient in a tough economy.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wallace Fixes Latin American Flavor Issues

Chairman and CEO Noel Wallace successfully steered the company through a challenging recovery in Latin America following a previous product misstep. The region saw a major rebound after the company reformulated a new flavor of Colgate Total that had initially caused irritation for some consumers.

"We are pleased to have exited 2025 with accelerated growth momentum on both the top and bottom lines, even in the face of sluggish category growth in many markets," Wallace said. He noted that the company is now operating from a position of strength as it transitions to a new 2030 growth strategy focused on science-based innovation and AI-driven data analytics.

Colgate's Oral Care Dominance Drives Global Results

The company maintained its crushing lead in the global toothpaste market, ending the year with a 41.3% market share. Manual toothbrushes also performed well, securing 32.4% of the global market. Despite these wins, the company faced headwinds in its pet nutrition segment, where a move away from private label products had a negative impact on volume.

However, the core business grew in every category during the quarter, proving that even as consumer sentiment wanes in the U.S., shoppers are still prioritizing basic hygiene and pet health.

Colgate-Palmolive Sets Cautious 2026 Outlook

Looking ahead, the company issued a conservative forecast for the new year, citing a difficult operating environment and slower category growth. Management expects organic sales to grow between 1% and 4% in 2026, which accounts for the impact of exiting the private label pet food business.

In addition, the company warned of a $794 million hit to its skin health division, specifically the Filorga brand, due to weak performance in China. To offset these pressures, Colgate plans to increase its advertising spend while leaning on its record $4.2 billion in annual operating cash flow to continue returning value to its shareholders.

Is Colgate-Palmolive a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to TipRanks, Colgate-Palmolive stock (CL) has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on nine Buys and five Holds assigned in the last three months. The average 12-month CL price target sits at $88.77, implying a downside risk of 1.3%.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: XOM Earnings: ExxonMobil Stock Dips Despite Q4 Earnings Beat as Oil Prices Weigh
data.description:
ExxonMobil (XOM) reported its full-year 2025 results on Friday, showcasing a year of record-breaking production even as market prices cooled. The energy giant earned $28.8 billion for the year, down from $33.7 billion in 2024. For the fourth quarter, adjusted earnings came in at $1.71 per share, narrowly beating the Wall Street consensus of $1.68. Despite the beat, shares slipped about 2.8% in early trading as the market reacted to the broader impact of lower commodity prices and chemical margins.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Woods Drives Profits Despite Lower Oil Prices

Chairman and CEO Darren Woods emphasized that the company is more resilient than in previous years, pointing to a strategy that prioritizes high-value production over simple volume. "ExxonMobil is a fundamentally stronger company than it was just a few years ago, and our 2025 results demonstrate that," Woods said.

Furthermore, Woods credited this performance to "advantaged assets, disciplined capital allocation, and execution excellence," which have allowed the firm to capture more value from every barrel produced. He further noted that this transformation is delivering a more resilient and technology-led business grounded in structurally stronger earnings power.

Guyana and the Permian Drive Record Production

The company's growth was largely powered by its two most profitable regions, where net production reached its highest level in over 40 years, averaging 4.7 million oil-equivalent barrels per day for the year. Production in the Permian Basin hit a record 1.6 million barrels per day, while Guyana output exceeded 700,000 gross barrels per day, supported by the Yellowtail development starting up four months ahead of schedule.

Additionally, global refining throughput reached its highest annual level since the 1999 merger of Exxon and Mobil, providing a strong downstream anchor for the company's record upstream output.

ExxonMobil Rewards Shareholders While Cutting Costs

Even with lower total earnings, ExxonMobil led the industry in returning cash to its investors by distributing $37.2 billion to shareholders in 2025 through a combination of dividends and share buybacks. The board declared a first-quarter dividend of $1.03 per share, maintaining its 43-year streak of annual growth. The financial cushion for these payments comes from an aggressive cost-reduction plan where the firm has slashed $15.1 billion in structural costs since 2019, a figure higher than all other major international oil companies combined. Management expects these savings to reach $20 billion by 2030.

Is ExxonMobil a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to TipRanks, ExxonMobil stock (XOM) has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 12 Buys and seven Holds assigned in the last three months. The average 12-month XOM price target sits at $135.11, implying a downside risk of 3.8%.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Dow Jones Dips as Trump Nominates Kevin Warsh to Replace Fed Chair Powell
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is set to open Friday in the red after President Trump announced former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh as his pick to succeed Jerome Powell. Powell's term as Chair is set to end on May 15, although his term as a Governor extends until January 31, 2028.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"I am pleased to announce that I am nominating Kevin Warsh to be the CHAIRMAN OF THE BOARD OF GOVERNORS OF THE FEDERAL RESERVE SYSTEM," Trump said in a Truth Social post.

Who is Kevin Warsh?

Former President George W. Bush nominated Warsh to join the Fed in 2006. At 35, Warsh became the youngest person to serve on the central bank's board.

Warsh is known in financial circles as a "hawk," or someone who favors tighter monetary policy, prioritizes controlling inflation, and generally supports higher interest rates even at the risk of slowing economic growth. That may come as a surprise to some, as Trump has repeatedly called for lower rates.

However, Warsh has advocated for rate cuts in recent months while voicing support for Trump's tariffs. He has also echoed Trump's criticism of Powell. Before assuming the role, Warsh must clear the Senate Banking Committee and then be confirmed by the Senate.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Tesla Stock (TSLA) Climbs as "Musk Metropolis" Merger Talks Gather Pace
data.description:
Shares in EV maker Tesla (TSLA) were higher today as analysts talked up the prospect of a super "Musk Metropolis" merger between Elon Musk's SpaceX and xAI companies.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Merger Mania

It was reported yesterday that SpaceX, home to rockets and Starlink satellites, as well as the X social media platform and xAI's Grok chatbot could come under one company ahead of a major public offering planned for later this year.

Under the proposed deal, xAI shareholders would receive SpaceX shares, and two new entities have already been created in Nevada to help facilitate the transaction. However, the exact value, timing, and structure of the merger are still unclear, and no final agreement has been signed.

The prospect is a tantalizing one given the complementary nature of the technologies involved. Through xAI, Musk is building a massive AI supercomputer called Colossus and could use SpaceX rockets to put AI data centers in space.
data.author: David Craik
